items.title: Somalia: Saudi Arabia Executes Three Somali Nationals Convicted of Hashish Smuggling
items.description: [Shabelle] Riyadh -- Saudi Arabia has on Sunday executed three Somali nationals convicted of smuggling hashish into the kingdom, the Interior Ministry said, as the Gulf state continues to carry out capital punishment for drug-related offences despite mounting criticism from human rights groups.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Saudi Arabia Executes Three Somali Nationals Convicted of Hashish Smuggling

items.title: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu
items.description: The European Union is imposing new restrictions on visas for Somali nationals after a disagreement with Mogadishu over the return of irregular migrants to their home country.
items.titleIt: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu

items.title: Top Somali official deported from Kenya over suspected passport fraud
items.description: Somalia’s Second Deputy Prime Minister was deported from Kenya on Thursday over allegations of passport fraud, according to numerous reports.
items.titleIt: Top Somali official deported from Kenya over suspected passport fraud

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026
items.description: In this online event, Crisis Group experts discussed peace and security priorities for the African continent in 2026, zooming in on Somalia, Sudan and South Sudan ahead of the African Union's summit of heads of state.
items.titleIt: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026

items.title: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 30 GIU - The Ethiopian and Italian governments have signed a 70-million-euro financing package to support the macroeconomic reforms underway in Ethiopia and the country’s sustainable development agenda. The agreement was formally signed by Ethiopian Finance Minister Ahmed Shide and the Italian Ambassador to Ethiopia, Sem Fabrizi. The financing package, a testament to the strength and depth of the historic partnership between Ethiopia and Italy—founded on mutual trust and shared development priorities within the framework of the Mattei Plan for Africa— includes a €70 million c
items.titleIt: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development

items.title: Sudan: How the UAE Is Fuelling the RSF Through Libya
items.description: [Ayin Network] A sprawling, covert network of military bases, complex logistics, and weapons trafficking routes operating out of eastern Libya has been exposed as a central artery sustaining the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and the ongoing war in Sudan.
items.titleIt: Sudan: How the UAE Is Fuelling the RSF Through Libya

items.title: Ethiopia: TPLF Rejects EU Criticism, Says Govt Violating Peace Deal
items.description: [Reporter] The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has rejected European Union accusations that it is undermining Ethiopia's 2022 peace agreement, instead accusing the federal government of violating the accord and warning that the Tigray region faces a growing risk of renewed conflict.

items.title: Sudan: China Forgives $50m Sudan Debt
items.description: [Dabanga] Port Sudan -- Sudan's Ministry of Finance has signed an agreement with China to cancel part of the country's debt, with Beijing agreeing to write off four interest-free loans owed by Sudan worth a total of 344.52m Chinese yuan (CNY), equivalent to about $50m. The agreement was signed in Port Sudan by Sudan's finance minister, Dr Jibril Ibrahim, on behalf of the Sudanese government, and by Xu Jian, China's chargé d'affaires in Sudan, on behalf of the Chinese government.
items.titleIt: Sudan: China Forgives $50m Sudan Debt
